title: centaurea alba subsp. subciliaris
intro: Centaurea alba subsp. subciliaris, also known as White Knapweed, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of White Knapweed include its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome and arthritis. Herbal preparations of White Knapweed can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, allowing for a variety of uses in traditional and modern herbal medicine.
